The Eight Day Countdown.name: The Eight Day Countdown
The Eight Day Countdown.description: The third floor has only eight days before it collapses. Crawlers must find stairwells and descend to the fourth floor before time runs out. The countdown began when the second floor collapsed, and the system announced over 700,000 crawlers remaining.
objectives: Reach the fourth floor before deadline
The Eight Day Countdown.consequences: Death if not descended before timer expires. The floor literally collapses.

events.event: Day 4: Party positioned at warehouse stairwell. Awaiting first magic pulse to activate handle.

Race and Class Selection.name: Race and Class Selection
Race and Class Selection.description: Upon entering Floor 3, crawlers must choose a race and class. Races include Primal (ancient, mysterious beings), while classes like Compensated Anarchist, Bomb Squad Tech, and Prize Fighter are available based on achievements and view counts. The Desperado Club tattoo excludes Cleric/Paladin classes.
Race and Class Selection.consequences: Class choice affects all future abilities and playstyle. Cannot be changed.

The Volcano World Mythology.name: The Volcano World Mythology
The Volcano World Mythology.description: Floor 3 is set in 'The Over City' - the ruins of a magical civilization that existed inside a dormant volcano. Six layers exist within the volcano. A great monster at the bottom breathed poison gas, killing or transforming 90% of the population. Survivors live in villages while monsters roam the ruins.
objectives: Explore the ruins for loot and experience
The Volcano World Mythology.consequences: Breaking village rules means death penalty with no jail - permanent exile to the ruins.

Grimaldi's Traveling Circus.name: Grimaldi's Traveling Circus
Grimaldi's Traveling Circus.description: A legendary circus transformed by the cataclysm. Ringmaster Redstone Grimaldi became a Level 85 City Boss - a Pestiferous Vine that keeps resurrecting his circus family forever. No crawler has survived fighting a city boss. The circus contains clowns, lemurs, ogres, and other transformed performers.
objectives: Decide whether to engage or flee
Find a way to defeat the city boss (if possible)
Grimaldi's Traveling Circus.consequences: City boss fights have zero survivor rate. Grimaldi resurrects his family regardless of how many times they die.

events.event: Freed Prometheus the dragon from captivity. Burned down the Big Top. Grimaldi wounded but will resurrect - he remains a threat.

The Murder Mystery Quest.name: The Murder Mystery Quest
The Murder Mystery Quest.description: Prostitutes from outside the city are falling from the sky, twisted to death with talon wounds on their shoulders - suggesting skyfowl involvement. GumGum the orc gave Carl this quest before being killed by krasue (ghost ladies with exposed guts). The 201st Security Group may be involved.
The Murder Mystery Quest.consequences: GumGum died for asking questions. The killers know Carl and Donut are investigating.

The Apito Doomsday Cult.name: The Apito Doomsday Cult
The Apito Doomsday Cult.description: A sect of banished elves worship the Oak Mother Apito, goddess of all gods. They believe skyfowl are 'blessed angels' whose protection maintains the path to heaven. They hunt the 'Oak Fell' (their antichrist) and believe Carl is it. They tortured GumGum for information and operate through the 201st Security Group.
objectives: Learn about the Oak Fell prophecy
Survive the cult's attempts to kill Carl
Understand the connection to Floor 12 events
The Apito Doomsday Cult.consequences: The cult believes killing Carl will prevent the end of all worlds. They will not stop hunting him.

The Skull Empire Conflict.name: The Skull Empire Conflict
The Skull Empire Conflict.description: A galactic political struggle between the Skull Empire, Valtay Corporation, and Borant Syndicate. The Valtay destroyed a Skull Empire royal yacht, killing Queen Consort Ugloo and the Maestro. This weakens alliances and affects the bankruptcy action against Earth's dungeon.
The Skull Empire Conflict.consequences: Galactic politics directly affect dungeon rules and crawler survival rates.

The Remex Soul Crystal Explosion.name: The Remex Soul Crystal Explosion
The Remex Soul Crystal Explosion.description: Remex contains an overloaded soul crystal that creates a city-destroying explosion when combined with a Sheol Glass Reaper Case. Carl contained one explosion in his inventory (3/1x10^7 power) but Remex still has a 20-minute timer to a second, larger explosion that will kill everyone in the area.
objectives: Find a way to contain or escape the blast
Get to the stairwell before explosion
The Remex Soul Crystal Explosion.consequences: City-leveling explosion. Magic pulses disable equipment before the final blast.

Magic Pulse Cascade.name: Magic Pulse Cascade
Magic Pulse Cascade.description: Before the floor collapse, magic pulses sweep through the area at intervals. First pulse disables stat buffs. Second pulse activates all equipped magical weapons. Third pulse destroys any still-equipped armor. Fourth pulse triggers first two items in everyone's hotlist automatically. Items in inventory become inaccessible.
objectives: Prepare for loss of magical items
Magic Pulse Cascade.consequences: Complete loss of magical gear. Random activation can heal or harm. Armor destroyed. Chaos ensues.

events.event: Party preparing: magical weapons/armor stowed in inventory. Mordecai identified risk — second pulse may activate Sheol Glass case. Two contingencies: stairwell shielding or Tandy body-absorbs the pulse.

The Desperado Club.name: The Desperado Club
The Desperado Club.description: A members-only criminal organization accessible via secret tattoo. The club exists on a separate dimensional plane - entering any location takes you to the same room. Contains: Bitches (female brothel), Penis Parade (male brothel), Jobs (assassination/theft quests), Silk Road (marketplace), Guild-O-Rama (rogue guilds). Stairs lead to Hunting Grounds (accessible from Floor 6).
The Desperado Club.consequences: Crawlers who enter Bitches/Penis Parade get shanked and ridiculed by viewers. Jobs quests are morally questionable.

The Skyfowl Magistrate.name: The Skyfowl Magistrate
The Skyfowl Magistrate.description: Magistrate Featherfall rules the eagle-controlled villages from a position above a high-end shopping mall. Skyfowl view flightless creatures as servants. The bureaucracy includes human and elf clerks who are verbally abused. Getting an audience requires navigating guards, shops, and a reception desk with a Beanie Baby-obsessed secretary.
The Skyfowl Magistrate.consequences: Skyfowl are arrogant and dismissive. Violence against them triggers the Apito cult response.

Crawler Alliance Formation.name: Crawler Alliance Formation
Crawler Alliance Formation.description: Hekla leads Brynhild's Daughters, an all-female Icelandic crawler team. She achieved Level 25 early and has an automatic magical crossbow. Her team was scattered on Floor 3 but regrouped to take down an owlbear borough boss. She sent a friend to find Carl and Donut at the One-Eyed Narwhal tavern.
Crawler Alliance Formation.consequences: Alliances provide safety in numbers but also make you a bigger target. Viewer dynamics change with team composition.

The Quest Completion System.name: The Quest Completion System
The Quest Completion System.description: Quests reward Gold/Platinum Quest Boxes upon completion. Completing quests in unusual ways triggers the 'Hadji' achievement, upgrading boxes. A double upgrade exists beyond Hadji. The 'Johnny Quest' achievement marks quest locations on your map. The system mocks crawlers with insulting achievement descriptions.
objectives: Find creative solutions for Hadji upgrades
The Quest Completion System.consequences: Better completion methods yield better rewards. Standard completion gets mocked by the system.

The Hobgoblin Explosives Trade.name: The Hobgoblin Explosives Trade
The Hobgoblin Explosives Trade.description: Pustule the hobgoblin sells explosives at the Silk Road. Hobgoblin dynamite is significantly more powerful than goblin explosives. Available items include: hob-lobbers (impact-detonated), hobgoblin smoke curtains (superior to goblin smoke bombs), and magical detonators with 10km range. Carl's explosives handling skill affects yield.
The Hobgoblin Explosives Trade.consequences: Hobgoblin dynamite can kill most anything, but even a case wouldn't scratch a divine guardian.

NPCs as Former Crawlers.name: NPCs as Former Crawlers
NPCs as Former Crawlers.description: Some NPCs like Remex were once crawlers who became trapped in the dungeon system. Mordecai will be freed once crawlers reach Floor 4 and the dungeon ends. Donut expressed fear of becoming an NPC herself. NPCs remember their past lives but are bound to their roles.
objectives: Learn which NPCs were former crawlers
NPCs as Former Crawlers.consequences: Crawlers can become trapped as NPCs. Death is not the worst fate in the dungeon.

The Odette Interview Show.name: The Odette Interview Show
The Odette Interview Show.description: Crawlers participate in televised interviews between floors. The show includes crawler celebrities, galactic guests, and 'Danger Zone' segments. Production trailers float in Earth orbit and can be attacked. Viewer ratings affect crawler privileges and bonuses. Embarrassing moments (like Carl's nudity) are broadcast to the universe.
The Odette Interview Show.consequences: High ratings unlock better classes and rewards. Low ratings mean death. Everything is broadcast.

Floor Rules and Survival.name: Floor Rules and Survival
Floor Rules and Survival.description: Village guards only operate during daylight. Night brings greater danger. The ruins contain 'Corrupt Temples' where mobs spawn. Don't attack NPCs, rob stores, or get drunk in streets - only death penalty exists. Guards are slow but numerous. Running into ruins provides escape but permanent exile from that village.
objectives: Identify safe zones and danger areas
Floor Rules and Survival.consequences: Rule violations mean death or permanent exile. No second chances.

The Jai-Alai Xistera.name: The Jai-Alai Xistera
The Jai-Alai Xistera.description: Carl received a xistera (jai-alai throwing scoop) as a prize, along with the Cesta Punta skill from an Earth Hobby Potion. The basket appears wicker but is lightweight metal. Jai-alai balls can knock heads off. The skill allows throwing objects at incredible speed and accuracy.
The Jai-Alai Xistera.consequences: Unique ranged weapon capability. Skill knowledge from Earth hobbies can provide unexpected advantages.

The Ricky Joe Subplot.name: The Ricky Joe Subplot
The Ricky Joe Subplot.description: Ricky Joe is a one-armed dwarven child whose mother is perpetually pregnant. His large family of 15+ siblings lives next to the Apito Education Center. He got involved when Carl and Donut were fighting the 201st Security Group, carrying a severed arm and salamander corpse. His mother was in labor during the confrontation.
objectives: Consider consequences of combat near families
The Ricky Joe Subplot.consequences: Combat in residential areas risks harming innocents. NPCs have their own lives and concerns.

The Stairwell Escape.name: The Stairwell Escape
The Stairwell Escape.description: The floor ends when crawlers pull the handle at the bottom of the stairwell. The door features oversized kua-tin carvings. Reaching the stairwell before the final explosion requires navigating magic pulses that disable equipment, running without pants (Carl's armor was destroyed), and timing the descent with the warehouse bomb.
The Stairwell Escape.consequences: Pulling the handle ends Floor 3. Anyone not through dies. The transition is instantaneous.

events.event: Party on warehouse rooftop with Prometheus. Crowd below pacified. Katia present. Gear stowed for pulse prep. Plan: sprint inside on first pulse, shield case from second pulse.
